No Poverty.reason: The course content and objectives do not address poverty eradication or related economic vulnerabilities. No explicit or implicit links are present in the syllabus.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course does not mention food security, nutrition, sustainable agriculture, or any related targets. There is no evidence to support a score for this SDG.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course syllabus contains no information related to health, well-being, disease prevention, or access to healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: The course directly promotes global citizenship, cultural understanding, and language skills for international communication, aligning strongly with Target 4.7 on education for sustainable development and cultural diversity.

Gender Equality.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any specific content, objectives, or activities related to gender equality, women's empowerment, or discrimination.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no mention of water quality, sanitation, water management, or related environmental issues in the course description or schedule.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course content does not include any topics related to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's activity of students introducing their hometowns (attractions, food) indirectly supports sustainable tourism (Target 8.9) by promoting local culture and products.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course mentions Taiwan's 'technology industry' as a topic of international interest, providing a minimal, contextual link to industrial development and innovation (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The dedicated session on 'The Indigenous Peoples of Taiwan' directly promotes social and cultural inclusion, aligning with Target 10.2 to empower and promote the inclusion of all, irrespective of origin.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Course topics like 'Landscapes in Taiwan,' 'Festivals and Customs,' 'Sights in Taiwan,' and introducing one's hometown directly contribute to safeguarding cultural and natural heritage (Target 11.4).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any content related to sustainable consumption, production patterns, waste reduction, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: The course does not address climate change, its impacts, mitigation, or adaptation strategies in any part of its curriculum.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no mention of marine ecosystems, ocean health, sustainable fisheries, or any water-related environmental issues in the course.

Life on Land.reason: The course includes a session on 'Landschaften in Taiwan,' which, by focusing on natural landscapes, provides a minimal, inferred link to the conservation of terrestrial ecosystems (Target 15.1).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course fosters international understanding through collaboration with Japanese students and discussion of Taiwan's 'international politics,' indirectly contributing to peaceful and inclusive societies (Target 16.7).
